name: NIH R21 Exploratory / Developmental Research Grant
emphasis: High-risk, high-reward science. Preliminary data is NOT required and reviewers are briefed accordingly — penalizing a proposal for lacking it is a review error. The idea must be genuinely novel and exploratory in character. Reviewers look for: transformative potential, clear rationale, and a team capable of executing the feasibility study.
avoid: Presenting so much preliminary data that the proposal reads like a completed R01. Conservative, low-risk aims inappropriate for R21. Do not propose aims that would normally justify a full R01 — scope must match the 2-year, $275K ceiling.
rhetorical_tone: Bold, exploratory, speculative-but-grounded. Acknowledge uncertainty explicitly as part of the scientific question.

sections.name: Specific Aims
sections.notes: Same structure as R01 Specific Aims but shorter scope. State what you will explore and what success looks like at the end of 2 years.

sections.name: Research Strategy
sections.notes: Same three subsections as R01 (Significance, Innovation, Approach) but compressed. Approach should focus on feasibility demonstrations, not full mechanistic studies.

notes: R21 is appropriate when you have an innovative idea but insufficient data for an R01. It is not a stepping stone to be misused for incremental work.
